title: Zeltnera arizonica
intro: Zeltnera arizonica, also known as Arizona penstemon, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Zeltnera arizonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Arizona penstemon is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in poultices and salves to treat skin irritations and wounds.
uses: Zeltnera arizonica is a plant with limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, some sources suggest that it has been used by Native American communities for various purposes. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

The active constituents in this plant are primarily found in its leaves, stems, and roots, which contain various bioactive compounds.
